NVDA.reasoning: No valid breakout setup is forming. Range boundaries: NVDA established a recent consolidation zone from roughly $207-$216.83 (Apr 24-29) after a strong recovery rally. Today (Apr 30) price broke DOWN through $207 support with a close around $199.57 — a 4.6% collapse that breached the wiki's hard invalidation level ($208.20 close below before new high). This is a breakdown, not a breakout candidate. The wiki confirms: 'Close below $208.20 before new high invalidates thesis,' and the prior stop at $205.30 was already triggered in the previous run (-$264.65 lesson). Volume ratio: 1.52x 20-day average (technically meets the 1.5x threshold), but volume is confirming the BEARISH breakdown, not a bullish breakout. Daily BB width: 0.241 — no squeeze forming; volatility has already expanded to the downside. The squeeze is DONE on the bearish side, not coiling for a new bullish move. Hourly indicators show strong bearish pressure: hourly ADX -DI 38.45 vs +DI 11.92, hourly RSI-21 at 23.58 (deeply oversold but no reversal confirmation), hourly MACD histogram deeply negative at -1.9473. No consolidation base is forming — price is actively trending down on the hourly timeframe. The nearest support is the $199.00 hourly level (18 tests), and daily support at $197.62 pivot. Swing breakout strategy requires a QUIET, coiling chart with contracting BB width before the break — this chart is the opposite: already expanded volatility in a bearish breakdown. Waiting for price to stabilize and form a new base before any breakout signal can be identified. Lessons context: the lessons table shows 0% win rate on EMA pullback/dip-buys in the last 30 days on NVDA; breakout failures (fade-back-into-range) are the recent pattern. No position recommended until a clear consolidation forms.
NVDA.timeframe: no setup — monitor for 5-10 trading days for base formation
